description_long: This object is a concrete Jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. It has a trapezoidal cross-section with a wide, stable base and a narrower top, providing both strength and resistance to tipping. The surface is rough and weathered, showing signs of age and exposure, with visible aggregate and some minor chipping. Two metal lifting hooks are embedded on the top for transport and placement. The color is predominantly gray with darker patches and stains, indicating prolonged outdoor use. There are no visible reflective strips or warning signs, but faint markings and minor scuffs are present.
description: A weathered, gray concrete Jersey barrier with a rough surface, metal lifting hooks, and minor surface wear.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the trapezoidal profile and the top lifting hook.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length, rough surface texture, and two metal hooks on top.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, similar to view 0, with visible top hook and base shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the length, rough texture, and both top hooks.
